subquestions.text:
What is printed by the following code?
System.out.println("abc".substring(1));

solution.answer: bc
solution.explanation: substring(1) returns the string starting from index 1 to the end.

subquestions.text:
What is the value of b?
boolean b = true || (10 / 0 == 0);
choices: The code gives a type error

solution.answer: true
solution.explanation: Short-circuit OR (||) returns true if the first operand is true, skipping the second.

questions.question_text: Types, classes and generics in Java

solution.answer: Map m = new HashMap<>();
solution.explanation: The other options have syntax errors or type mismatches.

subquestions.text: Which method header correctly describes a method that takes a String and returns its length?

solution.answer: int length(String s)
solution.explanation: Correct return type and parameter type for a length method.

subquestions.text: Which statement about String in Java is correct?
choices: Calling toUpperCase changes the original String in place.
String cannot be compared with equals.

solution.answer: String objects are immutable.
solution.explanation: Strings cannot be changed after creation.
grading_points: Correct selection of 'String objects are immutable.'

subquestions.text: Given class Dog extends Animal, which assignment is valid?
choices: Dog d = (Dog) new Object(); is always safe.

solution.answer: Animal a = new Dog();
solution.explanation: Polymorphism allows assigning a subclass instance to a superclass reference.
grading_points: Correct selection of 'Animal a = new Dog();'

subquestions.text: What is the most precise return type for a method that might fail to find a Student?

solution.answer: Optional
solution.explanation: Optional is the standard way to handle potentially missing values.

subquestions.text: What does static mean in a method declaration?
choices: The method belongs to the class rather than one object.
The method can never be called.

solution.answer: The method belongs to the class rather than one object.
solution.explanation: Static methods are associated with the class type.
grading_points: Correct selection of 'The method belongs to the class rather than one object.'

questions.context:
In this task you show that you understand basic console input/output, loops, and string processing in Java.
You may use Scanner, StringBuilder, Character.toUpperCase and Character.toLowerCase.

subquestions.label: a

solution.answer: public static String readSentence(Scanner sc) { StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); while (sc.hasNext()) { String s = sc.next(); sb.append(s).append(" "); if (s.endsWith(".")) break; } return sb.toString().trim(); }
solution.explanation: Reads tokens until one ends with a period.

subquestions.label: b
subquestions.text:
Write a main method that uses readSentence and prints the sentence with the word order reversed. The first word in the result must start with an uppercase letter, and the last word must start with a lowercase letter. You may assume at least one word and no proper nouns.
Input: This is a test.
Output: Test a is this.

solution.answer: public static void main(String[] args) { Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in); String s = readSentence(sc); String[] words = s.replace(".", "").split(" "); List<String> list = Arrays.asList(words); Collections.reverse(list); ... }
solution.explanation: Splits, reverses, and adjusts casing.

questions.question_text: Arrays, ArrayList and higher-order style operations
questions.topic: Arrays, ArrayList and higher-order style operations

subquestions.label: a

solution.answer: public static List<Integer> squareEvens(List<Integer> xs) { List<Integer> res = new ArrayList<>(); for (int x : xs) { if (x % 2 == 0) res.add(x * x); } return res; }
solution.explanation: Iterates and filters even numbers.

subquestions.label: b

solution.answer: public static List<Integer> squareEvensStream(List<Integer> xs) { return xs.stream().filter(x -> x % 2 == 0).map(x -> x * x).collect(Collectors.toList()); }
solution.explanation: Uses stream API.

subquestions.label: c

solution.answer: public static <T> int countNull(List<T> xs) { int count = 0; for (T x : xs) { if (x == null) count++; } return count; }
solution.explanation: Counts null elements.

subquestions.label: d

solution.answer: public static <T> List<T> dedupeConsecutive(List<T> xs) { List<T> res = new ArrayList<>(); T last = null; for (T x : xs) { if (res.isEmpty() || !x.equals(last)) { res.add(x); last = x; } } return res; }
solution.explanation: Removes consecutive duplicates.

subquestions.label: e

solution.answer: Yes, it is true. Because duplicateEach doubles every element, dedupeConsecutive will always collapse the pair back to the original single element.
solution.explanation: Logic explanation.

solution.answer: public static int computePercentDiscount(int percent, int price) { return (int) Math.round((percent / 100.0) * price); }
solution.explanation: Uses double for precision.

solution.answer: public static void takeItem(int itemId, Map<Integer, Integer> inventory) { if (inventory.containsKey(itemId)) { int count = inventory.get(itemId); if (count > 1) inventory.put(itemId, count - 1); else inventory.remove(itemId); } }
solution.explanation: Updates map correctly.

subquestions.label: a

solution.answer: public static void insertWord(String word, WordTree tree) { WordTree curr = tree; for (char c : word.toCharArray()) { curr = curr.continuations.computeIfAbsent(c, k -> new WordTree(false)); } curr.endAllowed = true; }
solution.explanation: Traverses and creates nodes.

subquestions.label: b
subquestions.text: Explain why your insertWord method preserves the invariant that each character has at most one continuation from a node.

solution.answer: The Map structure ensures that each character key maps to exactly one WordTree instance, preventing multiple paths for the same character.
solution.explanation: Invariant explanation.

subquestions.label: c

solution.answer: public static List<String> allWords(WordTree tree) { List<String> res = new ArrayList<>(); collect(tree, "", res); return res; } private static void collect(WordTree t, String s, List<String> res) { if (t.endAllowed) res.add(s); for (var entry : t.continuations.entrySet()) collect(entry.getValue(), s + entry.getKey(), res); }
solution.explanation: Recursive collection.
